steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that caterpillar sounds are "directed primarily at predators." To support this, we need evidence that predators actually respond to — and are deterred by — those sounds, not just facts about the sounds themselves.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'frequency characteristics': describes acoustic properties of the sounds but says nothing about how predators react. ✗
  • Choice B — 'production consistency': shows the sounds are produced reliably, but consistency alone doesn't prove they target predators. ✗
  • Choice C — 'predators stop attacking': chickens and yellow warblers cease attacks when they hear the sounds, directly showing a defensive effect on predators. ✓
  • Choice D — 'species specificity': addresses which species produce the sounds, not how predators respond to them. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
